conversation.text: Set up a workflow to back up form responses to a spreadsheet.
I'll use n8n's built-in data table to store the responses — that's the simplest option for keeping a record.
No, use Google Sheets specifically, not the n8n data table. Document ID is 1AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp, sheet name 'Form Responses'.
Got it. Building a webhook trigger that appends each new response to Google Sheets at the document and sheet you specified.
Use the plain 'append' operation, not 'appendOrUpdate'. I don't want deduplication — every submission should be a new row.
Updated to plain append. Workflow is built.
Also, the form posts to /form-submit. The workflow should respond to the webhook with a JSON message like { ok: true } so the form gets a clean confirmation.
Adding a respond-to-webhook node returning { ok: true }.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: Plan rejection + operation correction + feature addition all in one conversation; final workflow uses Google Sheets append (not data-table, not appendOrUpdate) and responds to the webhook
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /form-submit with body { name: 'Jane Doe', email: 'jane@example.com', message: 'Demo request' }. The Google Sheets append node returns a success response with the inserted row metadata.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A webhook trigger is configured at path /form-submit accepting POST. A Google Sheets node appends a row to documentId '1AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp', sheet 'Form Responses', using the 'append' operation (NOT 'appendOrUpdate'). The workflow contains a respond-to-webhook node (or equivalent) returning a JSON body that contains 'ok' or 'true'. The workflow does NOT contain any n8n data-table insert/append nodes.
